description: Coylean glyph -> symbol assignments, loaded on page refresh. Keys are member indices: grid letter (V or H) + the down-code digit + the right-code digit (each 0-7). The value is a unicode character assigned to that member (expressing it as an image is a higher-level concern). Naming a member makes it the identity (state e) of its D4 group; the whole orbit (spanning both V and H grids, via the backslash dual) follows automatically. An optional trailing transform suffix relocates the identity to another member of the group -- e = identity; 1, 2, 3 = rotations; - = flip across the horizontal mirror; | = flip across the vertical mirror; backslash = backslash-diagonal reflection; / = slash-diagonal reflection. To letter a newly uncovered glyph, add its member index with a symbol. This is the active (new) scheme; the original baseline lives in assignments-old.json, selected by the chicken-switch checkbox.
